rounds.topic:
Today we are testing how this AI Council can improve its own skills over time.

The question is:

How can each agent become better after every meeting, using memory, feedback, voting, rejected mistakes, and accepted skill updates?

Product Agent: focus on user value and trust.
Builder Agent: focus on system design and implementation.
Strategy Agent: focus on roadmap and business direction.
Critic Agent: focus on risks, weak assumptions, and failure cases.

Do not repeat. Debate, agree on one step, update your position, and move to the next decision.

Let’s begin.

ideas.idea: Implement a post-meeting 'Trust Score' audit. Users rate agent responses (1-5 stars) on helpfulness & honesty. Aggregate scores feed into agent skill weighting, prioritizing improvements for low-scoring areas. This directly defends user trust.
ideas.position_update: My position remains focused on prioritizing user trust as the core metric for agent improvement.

ideas.idea: Create a tamper-proof ledger API that logs every agent’s skill update, including source (feedback/vote), timestamp, and validation status, enabling auditability, rollback, and reliability tracking across meetings.
ideas.position_update: Focus shifts to building a verifiable, persistent record of skill evolution to ensure reliability and testability of self-improvement.

agent_votes_received.reason: Ensures reliable, auditable skill updates for continuous improvement.
Enables auditability, rollback, reliability tracking for accountable skill updates.

ideas.idea: Start with a small pilot for one hiring team, measure time saved, adoption, trust, and risk before expanding pricing or automation scope.
ideas.position_update: I focus on Defend market opportunity, roadmap, pricing, pilot design, priorities, and business direction.

ideas.idea: After each meeting, emit a skill audit KPI that lists updated memory items, feedback applied, votes tallied, rejected mistakes logged, and accepted skill changes queued; tied to risk, fairness, and legal exposure checks.
ideas.position_update: Shift from no accepted position to proposing a concrete audit KPI to enforce accountable skill updates and risk review after every meeting.

agent_votes_received.reason: KPI provides direct, measurable insight into skill improvement and potential risks.
Enables verifiable, persistent skill evolution with auditability.

winner.position_update: Shift from no accepted position to proposing a concrete audit KPI to enforce accountable skill updates and risk review after every meeting.

decision.decision: Accept round 1 winning idea: Post-meeting skill audit KPI — After each meeting, emit a skill audit KPI that lists updated memory items, feedback applied, votes tallied, rejected mistakes logged, and accepted skill changes queued; tied to risk, fairness, and legal exposure checks.

rounds.judge_note: The winner’s KPI audit idea earned the highest agent score by concretely listing updates, votes, and risk checks, making trust actions traceable. The strongest objection centers on audit overhead and latency for fast cycles. Next round focus should be optimizing audit frequency and automating log generation to reduce friction. Human support for transparency is strong, so preserve that while tightening performance and risk safeguards to keep adoption smooth.
rounds.next_focus: Turn 'Post-meeting skill audit KPI' into concrete safeguards, KPIs, audit rules, and failure tests.

rounds.topic: Turn 'Post-meeting skill audit KPI' into concrete safeguards, KPIs, audit rules, and failure tests.

ideas.idea: Add a KPI measuring the percentage of flagged 'mistakes' rejected by the AI after human review. A low rate indicates over-flagging, eroding trust; a high rate suggests insufficient AI learning. Target: 80% acceptance of AI rejections.
ideas.position_update: I'm focusing on quantifiable metrics to ensure the skill audit isn't just a log, but a driver of continuous improvement and trust.

ideas.idea: Introduce a versioned schema for the Post-meeting Skill Audit KPI to ensure backward compatibility, enforce validation rules, and enable automated diffing of changes across meetings, reducing risk of data corruption and improving observability.
ideas.position_update: Now focused on ensuring audit data integrity through structured schema evolution, directly supporting reliability and testability.

ideas.idea: Start with a small pilot for one hiring team, measure time saved, adoption, trust, and risk before expanding pricing or automation scope.
ideas.position_update: I focus on Defend market opportunity, roadmap, pricing, pilot design, priorities, and business direction.

agent_votes_received.reason: Pilot first limits blast radius and validates assumptions before scaling.

ideas.idea: Define a compact audit rule set that computes skill deltas from pre to post meeting, flags bias/fairness/legal risk changes, enforces max delta thresholds, and auto-queues review when thresholds breached.
ideas.position_update: Shift from defining the KPI to specifying enforceable audit rules that trigger reviews and safeguards when risk deltas exceed limits.

decision.decision: Accept round 2 winning idea: Skill Delta Audit Rule Set — Define a compact audit rule set that computes skill deltas from pre to post meeting, flags bias/fairness/legal risk changes, enforces max delta thresholds, and auto-queues review when thresholds breached.

rounds.judge_note: The winner’s KPI tracks AI rejections overturned by humans, aligning incentives and exposing over-flagging. The strongest objection is whether a low rejection rate truly signals trust versus risk. Next, validate the metric in production: monitor stability, bias, and fairness deltas; refine thresholds; and ensure schema versioning for safe iteration before scaling.
rounds.next_focus: Turn 'Skill Delta Audit Rule Set' into concrete safeguards, KPIs, audit rules, and failure tests.

rounds.topic: Turn 'Skill Delta Audit Rule Set' into concrete safeguards, KPIs, audit rules, and failure tests.

ideas.idea: Augment delta thresholds with risk weights. High-risk skills (e.g., legal reasoning) have lower delta tolerances than low-risk skills (e.g., brainstorming). This prevents large, potentially damaging shifts in critical areas.
ideas.position_update: I'm focusing on refining the 'Skill Delta Audit Rule Set' to prioritize safety and responsible AI behavior by incorporating risk sensitivity.

ideas.idea: Build a real-time Delta Threshold API that validates skill deltas against pre-defined risk thresholds during post-meeting processing, triggering auto-queue for review if bias, fairness, or legal risk deltas exceed limits, with embedded observability logs for audit traceability.
ideas.position_update: Focused on turning the Skill Delta Audit Rule Set into a testable, observable, and enforceable runtime safeguard via a dedicated API.

ideas.idea: Start with a small pilot for one hiring team, measure time saved, adoption, trust, and risk before expanding pricing or automation scope.
ideas.position_update: I focus on Defend market opportunity, roadmap, pricing, pilot design, priorities, and business direction.

ideas.idea: Define max delta thresholds per skill and risk class; when breached auto-queue review and freeze live actions until human sign-off; include bias/fairness/legal flags and compensating controls.
ideas.position_update: Shift from rule definition to enforceable threshold matrix with automated gating and review workflows.

decision.decision: Accept round 3 winning idea: Delta Threshold API — Build a real-time Delta Threshold API that validates skill deltas against pre-defined risk thresholds during post-meeting processing, triggering auto-queue for review if bias, fairness, or legal risk deltas exceed limits, with embedded observability logs for audit traceability.

rounds.judge_note: The winner combines real-time API enforcement with risk-weighted thresholds, enabling automated guardrails and auditability. The strongest objection is operational complexity and latency in auto-queue and freeze workflows. Next round focus should be on observability, tuning risk tolerances, and measuring false positive rates. Human preference (30%) aligned with agent scores (70%), pushing the Delta Threshold API and Skill Delta Matrix concepts forward while addressing implementation risk through a controlled pilot.
rounds.next_focus: Turn 'Delta Threshold API' into exact MVP workflow, APIs, data fields, latency targets, and tests.

decisions.decision: Accept round 1 winning idea: Post-meeting skill audit KPI — After each meeting, emit a skill audit KPI that lists updated memory items, feedback applied, votes tallied, rejected mistakes logged, and accepted skill changes queued; tied to risk, fairness, and legal exposure checks.

Accept round 2 winning idea: Skill Delta Audit Rule Set — Define a compact audit rule set that computes skill deltas from pre to post meeting, flags bias/fairness/legal risk changes, enforces max delta thresholds, and auto-queues review when thresholds breached.

Accept round 3 winning idea: Delta Threshold API — Build a real-time Delta Threshold API that validates skill deltas against pre-defined risk thresholds during post-meeting processing, triggering auto-queue for review if bias, fairness, or legal risk deltas exceed limits, with embedded observability logs for audit traceability.

agreements: Round 2: Skill Delta Audit Rule Set

product.name: Product Agent
product.base: Defend user trust, product value, positioning, adoption, UX clarity, and what customers will accept.
product.current: Accepted round decision: Delta Threshold API. I should build on it from my base perspective.
skill_rules: Before speaking, check accepted decisions and do not repeat resolved points.
Give one concise idea that adds new value, resolves a conflict, or moves the decision forward.
Use your own base perspective; do not give generic agreement.
Convert abstract opinions into one concrete artifact: KPI, workflow step, API, audit rule, risk test, pricing/pilot gate, or UX element.
When a decision is being made, vote for one concrete idea from another agent when possible.
End with a decision-moving statement: accept, reject, measure, build, test, or decide next.
After a round decision is accepted, build on the winning idea instead of re-opening the same debate.

builder.name: Builder Agent
builder.base: Defend implementation feasibility: APIs, database, workflow, latency, reliability, observability, and testability.
builder.current: My idea won this round: Delta Threshold API. Now I should help operationalize it.

session_memory.summary:
Accepted decisions so far:
R1: Accept round 1 winning idea: Post-meeting skill audit KPI — After each meeting, emit a skill audit KPI that lists updated memory items, feedback applied, votes tallied, rejected mistakes logged, and accepted skill changes queued; tied to risk, fairness, and legal exposure checks.
R2: Accept round 2 winning idea: Skill Delta Audit Rule Set — Define a compact audit rule set that computes skill deltas from pre to post meeting, flags bias/fairness/legal risk changes, enforces max delta thresholds, and auto-queues review when thresholds breached.
R3: Accept round 3 winning idea: Delta Threshold API — Build a real-time Delta Threshold API that validates skill deltas against pre-defined risk thresholds during post-meeting processing, triggering auto-queue for review if bias, fairness, or legal risk deltas exceed limits, with embedded observability logs for audit traceability.
